All States · Iowa

Iowa Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
Compact Enacted - Not Yet Active

Reciprocity in Iowa for Dietitians

Iowa has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. Until implementation goes live, apply by endorsement through the state board.

Once active, compact participation should reduce or eliminate the need for a separate transfer application.

Endorsement fee
$120
Processing time
3 to 4 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Not listed
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 900 hours of board-approved supervised practice or an accredited dietetic internship.

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Iowa requires you to hold active CDR registration.

  3. Submit the Online Application

    Create an account on the DIAL online licensing portal, complete the dietitian application, and pay the $120 application fee.

  4. Provide Verification and Training

    Provide proof of your active CDR registration and complete verification of U.S. citizenship or lawful presence (SAVE program). Additionally, Iowa dietitians must complete Mandatory Reporter Training.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$120
Processing time
3 to 4 Weeks
Renewal fee
$120
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years + 30 CE hours

Required Documents

  • CDR Registration Verification: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Official Transcripts: Sent directly to the Board showing your conferred degree.
  • Verification of Lawful Presence: Documentation satisfying the SAVE program requirements for state licensure.

Board Contact

Board
Iowa Board of Dietetics
Department
Department of Inspections, Appeals, and Licensing (DIAL)
Phone
(515) 281-0254
Email
PLPublic@idph.iowa.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $120. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years + 30 CE hours.

Iowa Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does an Iowa dietitian license cost?

The initial cost is a nonrefundable $120 application fee.

When does the Iowa license need to be renewed?

The Iowa dietitian license is renewed biennially (every two years). The renewal fee is $120. A late fee of $60 is applied if renewed after the expiration date.

What are the continuing education requirements for Iowa?

Iowa requires 30 hours of dietetics-related continuing education (CE) during each two-year renewal cycle. First-time licensees are exempt from this requirement for their initial renewal.

Does Iowa have reciprocity for dietitians?

Iowa has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Iowa?

Most endorsement applications take 3 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Iowa while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
